back_translation: The men of Ephraim said to Gideon, 'Why have you done this to us? Why did you not call us when you went to fight against Midian?' They contended with him sharply.
But Gideon answered them, 'Now what have I done compared to you? Are not the gleanings of Ephraim better than the grape harvest of Abiezer?'
'God has delivered Oreb and Zeeb, the two princes of Midian, into your hand. What was I able to do compared to you?' When he said this, their anger toward him subsided.
Then Gideon and his 300 men came to the Jordan and crossed over, exhausted but still pursuing.
Gideon said to the men of Succoth, 'Please give some loaves of bread to my men, for they are exhausted, and I am still pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.'
But the leaders of Succoth asked, 'Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your possession, that we should give bread to your army?'
Gideon answered, 'For this, when the LORD has delivered Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will tear your flesh with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers.'
From there he went up to Penuel and asked the same thing of them, but the men of Penuel answered as the men of Succoth had answered.
So he said to the men of Penuel, 'When I return in triumph, I will tear down this tower!'
Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with their army of about 15,000 men — all that were left of the entire army of the people of the east, for 120,000 swordsmen had fallen.
Gideon went up by way of the caravan route east of Nobah and Jogbehah, and he struck the army, taking them by surprise.
Zebah and Zalmunna fled, and he pursued them and captured the two kings of Midian — Zebah and Zalmunna — routing the entire army.
Gideon son of Joash returned from battle by way of the Ascent of Heres.
There he captured a young man of Succoth and questioned him. The young man wrote down for him the leaders and elders of Succoth — 77 men.

Gideon went to the men of Succoth and said, 'Here are Zebah and Zalmunna, about whom you taunted me, saying, Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your possession, that we should give bread to your exhausted men?'
He took the elders of the city and, using the thorns and briers of the wilderness, he disciplined the men of Succoth.
He also pulled down the tower of Penuel and killed the men of the city.
Gideon asked Zebah and Zalmunna, 'What kind of men did you kill at Tabor?' They answered, 'Men like you — each one resembling a son of a king.'
Gideon said, 'They were my brothers, the sons of my own mother. As the LORD lives, if you had let them live, I would not kill you.'
He said to Jether his firstborn, 'Get up and kill them.' But the young man did not draw his sword, for he was afraid because he was still young.

Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, 'Get up yourself and strike us, for as the man is, so is his strength.' So Gideon arose and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took the crescent ornaments on the necks of their camels.
Then the men of Israel said to Gideon, 'Rule over us — you and your son and your grandson — for you have saved us from the hand of Midian.'
But Gideon said to them, 'I will not rule over you, nor will my son rule over you. The LORD will rule over you.'
Then Gideon said to them, 'Let me make a request of you, that each one give me an earring from his plunder.' (For they had gold earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.)
They answered, 'We will gladly give them.' So they spread out a garment, and each man threw onto it an earring from his plunder.

The weight of the gold earrings he had requested was 1,700 shekels, besides the crescent ornaments, the pendants, the purple garments worn by the kings of Midian, and the chains on the necks of their camels.
Gideon made an ephod from all this and placed it in his city in Ophrah. All Israel prostituted themselves by worshiping it there, and it became a snare to Gideon and to his household.
So Midian was subdued before the descendants of Israel and did not raise its head again. The land had rest for forty years in the days of Gideon.
Jerubbaal son of Joash went and dwelt in his house.
Gideon had 70 sons of his own offspring, for he had many wives.
His concubine who was in Shechem also bore him a son, and he named him Abimelech.
Gideon son of Joash died at a good old age and was buried in the tomb of his father Joash, in Ophrah of the Abiezrites.
As soon as Gideon died, the descendants of Israel turned and prostituted themselves with the Baals, and they set up Baal-berith as their god.
The descendants of Israel did not remember the LORD their God, who had delivered them from the hand of all their enemies around them.
They did not show kindness to the house of Jerubbaal (that is, Gideon) according to all the good he had done for Israel.
